concerns.analysis: According to the provided image manifest, all extracted images have a 'valid: false' status with the error 'No module named 'PIL''. This indicates that the base64 image data is corrupt or truncated. As per the instructions, images with 'valid: false' will not render in the browser and must be flagged as a critical failure. This includes crucial organizational logos on page 1 and the critical topographic site maps on pages 16 and 17.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1: Official letterhead for North Carolina Environmental Quality, featuring the state seal. Page 16: Topographical site map displaying contour lines.
concerns.output_quote: Images are present in the HTML as tags but the base64 data is corrupt/invalid according to the manifest, so they will appear broken.

concerns.analysis: On page 11 (Attachment 1, Page 1), the source PDF features a nested list under 'B. Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans.' This item contains a sub-list starting with '1. The information provided by Alamance Aggregates...', which itself contains a further sub-list ('a. Date and time...', 'b. GPS coordinates...', etc.). In the output JSON, the lettered sub-items ('a.', 'b.', 'c.') are rendered as siblings to '1.', rather than being nested underneath it. This constitutes hierarchy flattenin
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1. The information provided by Alamance Aggregates in each Blasting Plan submitted to Colonial and NCDEQ shall include all of the following:
a. Date and time of the proposed blast.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 4933 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=4933. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 28 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=28, examples=['mailto:report.spam@nc.gov', 'http://www.colpipe.com', 'http://www.colpipe.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (17): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=17. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "From:"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

The source document relies heavily on underlined text to denote specific sub-sections and emphasis (e.g., 'Certified Mail' on Page 1; 'Notice of Blasting Related Activities' on Page 11). The JSON output strips this underline formatting entirely.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A. Notice of Blasting Related Activities. [Underlined in source PDF]
concerns.output_quote: A. Notice of Blasting Related Activities. [No underline representation]
